title: Switching canonical identity signaling is contradictory
description: The Switching workstream says the identity-table reference is downgraded because the file is missing, the control board says the file is present, and the current worktree does not contain `tables/switching_canonical_identity.csv`. This creates contradictory operational state signaling around canonical identity for the active canonical module.

title: Non-canonical Aging and Relaxation are promoted as canonical or paper-ready
description: `analysis/unified_dynamical_crossover_synthesis.m` uses canonical and paper-ready wording for a cross-module story built from Aging and Relaxation sources even though both modules remain non-canonical in the status anchors and the control board explicitly warns against reading such wording as closure evidence.

title: Relaxation ver3 active-module coverage is only implicit
description: `docs/system_registry.json` lists `Relaxation ver3` as the active module token, but the canonicalization status tables only track `Relaxation`. Without an explicit token mapping in the main anchors, progress coverage for the active module name remains ambiguous.

title: MT ver2 coverage blocker text is stale
description: The MT coverage workstream row still says coverage rows were missing, but that row already exists and the module status table already records MT coverage-gap tracking. The blocker and next action therefore lag the current documented state.
